message:
feat(btree): add swizzle protocol module and E2E pilot test

Introduce the swizzle protocol for B-tree page pointer resolution,
with a new swizzle.rs module, updated btree lib.rs exports, design
documentation, and a pilot E2E test script.

- crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs: New module implementing page
  pointer swizzling for in-memory B-tree traversal optimization
- crates/fsqlite-btree/src/lib.rs: Add swizzle module export
- docs/design/: Add design documentation for the swizzle protocol
- docs/e2e-scenario-catalog.md: Add 6 new E2E scenario entries
- e2e/bd_2uza4_1_swizzle_protocol_pilot.sh: Pilot E2E test script
- Sync beads issue tracker metadata

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2uza4","title":"LeanStore / Pointer Swizzling (§15.3)","description":"Buffer manager hot-path optimization via pointer swizzling per the LeanStore design (Leis et al. 2018). Score 4.5 in the opportunity matrix. Expected 2-5x page access speedup by eliminating page-table indirection on the hot path.

## Background
Traditional buffer managers maintain a page table: a hash map from (file_id, page_id) to buffer frame pointer. Every page access requires a page table lookup, which involves: hash computation, hash table probe (potentially multiple cache misses for chaining), and a pin/unpin protocol. For hot-path operations (B-tree traversal), this overhead is significant — up to 30% of total execution time.

## Pointer Swizzling
Instead of storing page IDs in B-tree internal nodes, store direct pointers to buffer frames. When a page is loaded into the buffer pool:
1. 'Swizzle' all page ID references in the parent page to point directly to the buffer frame
2. Subsequent accesses through the parent skip the page table entirely — just follow the pointer
3. When a page is evicted, 'unswizzle' the pointer back to the original page ID

The key insight: most B-tree traversals follow hot paths where pages are already in the buffer pool. Swizzling makes these hot paths pointer-chase-fast (single memory access) instead of hash-table-probe-fast (multiple memory accesses).

## Key Paper
Leis, Haubenschild, Kemper, Neumann 2018: 'LeanStore: In-Memory Data Management Beyond Main Memory'

## Implementation Challenges
- Swizzle/unswizzle must be atomic with respect to concurrent readers
- Eviction must unswizzle before freeing the buffer frame
- Need a mechanism to distinguish swizzled pointers from page IDs (tag bit or separate flag)
- Root pages must always be swizzled (never evicted)
- Recovery must handle pages that were swizzled at crash time
- Page cooling/heating state machine controls eviction candidacy

## Integration Points
- S3-FIFO (bd-1xfri): Buffer pool eviction policy determines which pages to evict; swizzle state machine handles the swizzle/unswizzle lifecycle around eviction
- EBR (bd-2y306): Epoch-based reclamation protects swizzled pointer dereference during concurrent unswizzle operations
- Vectorized scan (bd-14vp7.2): Scans can skip page table entirely for swizzled pages, feeding batches faster

## Expected Impact
- 2-5x speedup for B-tree point lookups (eliminate page table overhead)
- Reduced cache pollution (page table entries no longer in cache)
- Benefit compounds with deeper B-trees (more levels = more page table lookups eliminated)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pointer swizzling protocol designed with concurrency safety analysis
- [ ] SwizzlePtr type with tag-bit encoding and atomic CAS
- [ ] Swizzle on page load, unswizzle on eviction
- [ ] Atomic swizzle/unswizzle with concurrent readers (EBR-protected)
- [ ] Page cooling/heating state machine (HOT/COOLING/COLD)
- [ ] Eviction correctly unswizzles before freeing
- [ ] Root pages pinned and always swizzled
- [ ] B-tree traversal transparently handles swizzled/unswizzled pointers
- [ ] Benchmark: B-tree point lookup speedup with swizzling (target 2-5x)
- [ ] Miri clean: no use-after-free

## Success Criteria
Epic completion requires all child beads to satisfy their structured acceptance criteria, including explicit unit/property coverage, deterministic e2e evidence, and structured logging/metrics validation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every child bead in this epic defines concrete unit/property test scope with deterministic pass/fail thresholds and invariant IDs. 2. Structured logging and metrics contracts are mandatory for all child outputs (trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, seed, timing, outcome, error context) and validated by CI conformance checks. 4. Epic completion requires a machine-readable evidence manifest linking child unit/e2e/log artifacts to acceptance outcomes, plus an explicit fallback/rollback plan that preserves concurrent-writer defaults.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:17.509974725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:14.082908873Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-concept","alien-pass-2026-02-13","buffer-pool","ev-gated","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uza4","depends_on_id":"bd-14vp7","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2uza4.1","title":"Design pointer swizzle/unswizzle protocol for B-tree pages","description":"Design the pointer swizzling protocol for B-tree internal page references following LeanStore (Leis et al. 2018). This is the foundational design task for the LeanStore epic.

## Design Requirements

1. Pointer representation: Define a tagged pointer type SwizzlePtr that can hold either a PageId (unswizzled, on-disk reference) or a *const BufferFrame (swizzled, in-memory direct pointer). Use the lowest bit as a tag (swizzled=1, unswizzled=0) since buffer frames are aligned.

2. Swizzle operation: When a page is loaded into the buffer pool, scan the parent page child pointers and replace any PageId matching the loaded page with a swizzled pointer to its buffer frame. Must be atomic with respect to concurrent readers (use AtomicU64 with compare_exchange).

3. Unswizzle operation: When a page is evicted from the buffer pool, scan all parent pages that reference the evicted page and replace swizzled pointers with the original PageId. Must prevent concurrent readers from following a dangling pointer during unswizzle. Use epoch-based protection (integrate with EBR from bd-2y306).

4. Root pinning: Root pages of each B-tree must always be swizzled (never evicted). Maintain a pinned set.

5. Cooling/heating states: Pages transition through HOT (swizzled, frequently accessed), COOLING (swizzled, candidate for eviction), COLD (unswizzled, evicted or never loaded).

6. Recovery: On crash, all in-memory swizzled pointers are lost. Recovery loads pages on demand (all start as unswizzled) and progressively re-swizzles as pages are accessed.

## Alien CS Reference
Leis, Haubenschild, Kemper, Neumann 2018 LeanStore In-Memory Data Management Beyond Main Memory: Sections 3.1-3.3 detail the swizzle protocol, cooling state machine, and unswizzle-under-concurrency guarantee. Graefe 2011 Modern B-Tree Techniques: Section 4.5 discusses buffer pool bypass via pointer swizzling in the context of B-tree optimizations.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Define SwizzlePtr as repr(transparent) wrapper around AtomicU64 for zero-cost abstraction
- Tag bit encoding: bit 0 = swizzled flag; upper 63 bits = PageId or frame pointer (both naturally aligned)
- Use Ordering::AcqRel for CAS operations on SwizzlePtr
- Design document should be a Markdown file in docs/design/ with diagrams (Mermaid state machine diagram for cooling states)
- Prototype the SwizzlePtr type in btree/swizzle.rs with comprehensive unit tests

## Logging Requirements
- Log swizzle/unswizzle events at tracing::trace level (high frequency, trace only)
- Log state transitions (HOT->COOLING->COLD) at tracing::debug level
- Log root page pinning decisions at tracing::info level
- Log design document location and review status

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Design document with node format, memory layout, and iterator API
- [ ] Concurrency safety analysis (how readers are protected during unswizzle)
- [ ] Integration points with buffer pool and EBR documented
- [ ] Recovery protocol specified
- [ ] Prototype SwizzlePtr type with tag-bit encoding
- [ ] Mermaid state machine diagram for cooling states","acceptance_criteria":"1. Design artifact defines state machine/data model, explicit invariants, failure modes, and deterministic fallback behavior with clear ownership boundaries. 2. Unit tests validate schema/state-transition invariants derived directly from the design specification. 3. A pilot end-to-end scenario proves the design contract in integrated execution and emits replayable artifacts. 4. Logging contract for the design specifies required structured fields and automated conformance checks to catch drift early.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:19:56.635911959Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:10.869752999Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uza4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2uza4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-2uza4.1","title":"Design pointer swizzle/unswizzle protocol for B-tree pages","description":"Design the pointer swizzling protocol for B-tree internal page references following LeanStore (Leis et al. 2018). This is the foundational design task for the LeanStore epic.

## Design Requirements

1. Pointer representation: Define a tagged pointer type SwizzlePtr that can hold either a PageId (unswizzled, on-disk reference) or a *const BufferFrame (swizzled, in-memory direct pointer). Use the lowest bit as a tag (swizzled=1, unswizzled=0) since buffer frames are aligned.

2. Swizzle operation: When a page is loaded into the buffer pool, scan the parent page child pointers and replace any PageId matching the loaded page with a swizzled pointer to its buffer frame. Must be atomic with respect to concurrent readers (use AtomicU64 with compare_exchange).

3. Unswizzle operation: When a page is evicted from the buffer pool, scan all parent pages that reference the evicted page and replace swizzled pointers with the original PageId. Must prevent concurrent readers from following a dangling pointer during unswizzle. Use epoch-based protection (integrate with EBR from bd-2y306).

4. Root pinning: Root pages of each B-tree must always be swizzled (never evicted). Maintain a pinned set.

5. Cooling/heating states: Pages transition through HOT (swizzled, frequently accessed), COOLING (swizzled, candidate for eviction), COLD (unswizzled, evicted or never loaded).

6. Recovery: On crash, all in-memory swizzled pointers are lost. Recovery loads pages on demand (all start as unswizzled) and progressively re-swizzles as pages are accessed.

## Alien CS Reference
Leis, Haubenschild, Kemper, Neumann 2018 LeanStore In-Memory Data Management Beyond Main Memory: Sections 3.1-3.3 detail the swizzle protocol, cooling state machine, and unswizzle-under-concurrency guarantee. Graefe 2011 Modern B-Tree Techniques: Section 4.5 discusses buffer pool bypass via pointer swizzling in the context of B-tree optimizations.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Define SwizzlePtr as repr(transparent) wrapper around AtomicU64 for zero-cost abstraction
- Tag bit encoding: bit 0 = swizzled flag; upper 63 bits = PageId or frame pointer (both naturally aligned)
- Use Ordering::AcqRel for CAS operations on SwizzlePtr
- Design document should be a Markdown file in docs/design/ with diagrams (Mermaid state machine diagram for cooling states)
- Prototype the SwizzlePtr type in btree/swizzle.rs with comprehensive unit tests

## Logging Requirements
- Log swizzle/unswizzle events at tracing::trace level (high frequency, trace only)
- Log state transitions (HOT->COOLING->COLD) at tracing::debug level
- Log root page pinning decisions at tracing::info level
- Log design document location and review status

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Design document with node format, memory layout, and iterator API
- [ ] Concurrency safety analysis (how readers are protected during unswizzle)
- [ ] Integration points with buffer pool and EBR documented
- [ ] Recovery protocol specified
- [ ] Prototype SwizzlePtr type with tag-bit encoding

 {"id":"bd-2uza4.2","title":"Implement SwizzlePtr and B-tree swizzle/unswizzle operations","description":"Implement the core pointer swizzling mechanism for B-tree internal pages per the LeanStore design (Leis et al. 2018).

## Implementation

1. SwizzlePtr type (in btree/swizzle.rs): Tagged pointer using AtomicU64. Bit 0 = swizzled flag. Unswizzled: PageId in upper 63 bits. Swizzled: *const BufferFrame in upper 63 bits (aligned, so bit 0 is free). Methods: is_swizzled(), as_page_id(), as_frame_ptr(), swizzle(frame_ptr), unswizzle() -> PageId.

2. Swizzle on page load: When BufferPool::fix_page(page_id) loads a page into a frame, CAS the parent child pointer from unswizzled PageId to swizzled *const BufferFrame. If CAS fails (concurrent swizzle), retry or accept (idempotent).

3. Unswizzle on eviction: When BufferPool::evict_page(frame) evicts a page, pin the current EBR epoch (prevent concurrent readers from using the frame after free), CAS the parent child pointer from swizzled to unswizzled, defer frame deallocation until epoch advances (EBR safety).

4. B-tree traversal integration: BTree::search(key) handles both pointer types. Swizzled: follow pointer directly (fast path, no page table lookup). Unswizzled: call BufferPool::fix_page(page_id) (slow path, triggers swizzle).

5. Optimistic traversal: Use optimistic latch coupling (read version counter, traverse, validate) to avoid acquiring latches on internal nodes. Only the leaf node needs a latch for modification.

## Alien CS Reference
Leis et al. 2018 LeanStore: Section 3.2 details the SwizzlePtr implementation with atomic CAS protocol. Levandoski et al. 2013 BW-Tree: alternative approach using mapping table indirection (SwizzlePtr eliminates this indirection for better cache behavior). The optimistic latch coupling follows Leis et al. 2016 The ART of Practical Synchronization.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- SwizzlePtr must be Send + Sync (uses AtomicU64 internally)
- No raw pointer dereference without epoch protection: wrap in unsafe block with safety comment referencing crossbeam::epoch::Guard lifetime
- Use crossbeam-epoch::Guard to protect frame pointer dereference
- Implement Debug for SwizzlePtr showing swizzle state and contained value
- cfg(test) with Miri to detect use-after-free: #[cfg(miri)] tests with reduced iteration counts
- Unit test: swizzle/unswizzle roundtrip preserves PageId using proptest for random PageIds

## Logging Requirements
- Log swizzle operations at tracing::trace level: page_id, frame_ptr, CAS result
- Log unswizzle operations at tracing::trace level: page_id, old_frame_ptr
- Log CAS failures (contention) at tracing::debug level with retry count
- Log epoch-deferred deallocations at tracing::debug level

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SwizzlePtr type with tag-bit encoding and atomic operations
- [ ] Swizzle on page load with CAS
- [ ] Unswizzle on eviction with EBR protection
- [ ] B-tree traversal handles both swizzled/unswizzled pointers
- [ ] Unit tests: swizzle/unswizzle roundtrip preserves PageId
- [ ] Miri clean: no use-after-free under concurrent access
- [ ] Benchmark: swizzled B-tree point lookup vs unswizzled (expected 2-5x faster)","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. End-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include deterministic replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated so failures are diagnosable and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:20:13.299657725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:10.522869002Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uza4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2uza4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2uza4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2uza4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2uza4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2y306.1","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2uza4.3","title":"Implement page cooling/heating state machine with eviction integration","description":"Implement the page cooling/heating state machine from LeanStore that integrates pointer swizzling with buffer pool eviction (S3-FIFO from bd-1xfri).

## State Machine

Pages transition through three states:
1. HOT: Swizzled, actively accessed. Cannot be evicted.
2. COOLING: Swizzled but not recently accessed. Candidate for eviction. A background thread periodically moves HOT pages with low access frequency to COOLING.
3. COLD: Unswizzled, either evicted or never loaded. On-disk only.

Transitions: COLD -> HOT (page loaded via fix_page, swizzled into parent). HOT -> COOLING (background cooling thread marks low-frequency pages). COOLING -> HOT (page accessed again while in COOLING, re-heated). COOLING -> COLD (page evicted by S3-FIFO, unswizzled from parent).

## Cooling Thread
Background thread runs every COOLING_INTERVAL (default 100ms). Scans buffer pool frames, checks access counters. Pages with access_count < COOLING_THRESHOLD since last scan: transition HOT -> COOLING. Access counter uses AtomicU32 with Relaxed ordering (approximate is fine).

## Integration with S3-FIFO
S3-FIFO manages eviction ordering (which pages to evict). Swizzle state machine manages swizzle/unswizzle lifecycle. When S3-FIFO selects a page for eviction: if HOT skip (should not happen if cooling is working); if COOLING unswizzle then evict; if COLD already evicted.

## Root Pinning
Root pages of each B-tree index are permanently pinned in HOT state. Maintained in a PinnedSet: HashSet<PageId> checked during cooling scan.

## Alien CS Reference
Leis et al. 2018 LeanStore: Section 3.3 describes the cooling/heating state machine and background cooling thread. The state machine design follows the principle of separating eviction policy (S3-FIFO) from eviction mechanism (swizzle state transitions). Stoica and Ailamaki 2013: background page management in modern buffer pools.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Define enum PageThermalState { Hot, Cooling, Cold } with AtomicU8 storage for lock-free state transitions
- Background cooling thread: use std::thread::spawn with crossbeam::channel for shutdown signaling
- Access counter: AtomicU32 per buffer frame, incremented on access with Relaxed ordering
- PinnedSet: use dashmap::DashSet<PageId> for concurrent access from cooling thread and fix_page
- State transition: use compare_exchange on AtomicU8 to prevent TOCTOU races
- Integration test: use loom crate for model-checking concurrent state transitions (optional, high value)

## Logging Requirements
- Log state transitions at tracing::debug level: page_id, from_state, to_state, access_count
- Log cooling thread scan summary at tracing::info level: pages_scanned, pages_cooled, pages_reheated
- Log root page pinning at tracing::info level: page_id, btree_name
- Log eviction-state mismatch (evicting HOT page) at tracing::warn level

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] State machine with HOT/COOLING/COLD transitions
- [ ] Background cooling thread with configurable interval and threshold
- [ ] Root page pinning
- [ ] Integration with S3-FIFO eviction decisions
- [ ] Access counter tracking with atomic operations
- [ ] Unit test: page lifecycle through all state transitions
- [ ] Stress test: concurrent access + cooling + eviction without crashes","acceptance_criteria":"1. Implementation is complete and behaviorally correct against explicit invariants and edge-case requirements, including fault and recovery paths where applicable. 2. Comprehensive unit/property tests cover normal, boundary, and adversarial conditions with deterministic fixtures and regression seeds. 3. End-to-end scenario scripts validate integrated behavior in real execution flows and include deterministic replay instructions. 4. Structured logging/metrics (traceable IDs, timing, counters, error context) are emitted and validated so failures are diagnosable and auditable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:20:26.313736053Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:06:10.164631198Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2uza4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1xfri.1","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2uza4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2uza4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2uza4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2uza4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:00Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2uza4.4","title":"Benchmark: swizzled vs unswizzled B-tree point lookup throughput","description":"Benchmark the performance impact of pointer swizzling on B-tree point lookups to validate the expected 2-5x speedup from LeanStore-style buffer management.

## Workloads

1. Sequential point lookups: Look up N keys sequentially (tests cache-friendly traversal). N = 100K, 1M, 10M lookups. B-tree sizes: 10K, 100K, 1M, 10M rows.

2. Random point lookups: Look up N random keys (tests cache-unfriendly traversal). Same N and B-tree sizes. Uniform random and Zipfian distributions.

3. Mixed read-write: 90% point lookups + 10% inserts (tests swizzle/unswizzle churn). 32 concurrent threads. Measure impact of unswizzle overhead on read throughput.

4. Deep B-tree: Create an artificially deep B-tree (small page size, many levels). Tests the compounding benefit of swizzling (more levels = more page table lookups eliminated). Depths: 3, 5, 7, 10 levels.

## Metrics per workload
Throughput (lookups/second), latency (p50, p95, p99), CPU cycles per lookup (perf stat), L1/L2/L3 cache miss rates (perf stat), page table lookup count (counted via instrumented buffer pool), swizzle hit rate (percent of child pointer dereferences that were swizzled).

## Alien CS Reference
Leis et al. 2018 LeanStore: Section 5 presents benchmark methodology and expected speedup ranges for pointer swizzling. Their results show 2-5x for in-memory workloads. Haubenschild et al. 2020 Rethinking Logging, Checkpoints, and Recovery for High-Performance Storage Engines: extends LeanStore benchmarks with recovery-aware measurements.

## Rust Implementation Guidance
- Use criterion crate for statistical benchmarking with warmup and iteration control
- Use perf-event crate or perf stat subprocess for hardware counter collection
- Generate Zipfian keys with rand_distr::Zipf distribution
- Deep B-tree: set page_size to 256 bytes to force deeper trees without huge data
- Instrument buffer pool with conditional compilation: #[cfg(feature = benchmark-counters)] for page table lookup counting
- Output results as JSON for CI regression detection and chart generation

## Logging Requirements
- Log benchmark configuration at tracing::info level: workload type, N, B-tree size, thread count
- Log per-workload results at tracing::info level: throughput, p50/p95/p99 latency, swizzle hit rate
- Log cache miss rates at tracing::info level when perf counters available
- Log comparison summary (swizzled vs unswizzled speedup) at tracing::info level

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 4 workloads benchmarked
- [ ] Speedup chart: swizzled vs unswizzled per workload
- [ ] Cache miss rate comparison
- [ ] Swizzle hit rate documented
- [ ] CI integration for regression detection on SF1
- [ ] Results documented in benchmark report","acceptance_criteria":"1. Benchmark harness specifies workload, dataset shape, seed, and environment controls, and reports throughput plus p50/p95/p99 latency against a stable baseline. 2. Correctness guard tests (unit/property) run before performance assertions so regressions never trade correctness for speed. 3. An end-to-end smoke scenario validates the benchmarked path in integrated engine mode and verifies comparable outputs. 4. Benchmark logs include hardware/runtime config, query/workload IDs, sample distributions, and artifact paths for independent reproduction.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. @@ -0,0 +1,217 @@
+# Pointer Swizzle/Unswizzle Protocol for B-Tree Pages (bd-2uza4.1)
+
+## Scope
+
+This document defines the pointer swizzle protocol for B-tree child references:
+
+- Tagged pointer representation (`SwizzlePtr`).
+- Atomic swizzle/unswizzle transitions.
+- HOT/COOLING/COLD page residency state machine.
+- Concurrency safety with epoch-based reclamation (EBR).
+- Recovery behavior after process crash.
+- Structured logging contract for protocol observability.
+
+This is intentionally a design-first artifact with a small, testable Rust prototype in `crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs`.
+
+## Goals and Non-Goals
+
+Goals:
+- Replace selected `PageId` child references with direct frame pointers when safe.
+- Guarantee no dangling-pointer follow on unswizzle under concurrency.
+- Keep deterministic fallback available (`all unswizzled`) when swizzle path is disabled or degraded.
+
+Non-goals (this bead):
+- Full buffer-pool integration with parent back-reference indexes.
+- Full EBR wiring across all eviction paths.
+- Throughput tuning of the full swizzle lifecycle.
+
+## Data Model
+
+### Tagged Word Format
+
+`SwizzlePtr` is a `repr(transparent)` abstraction over `AtomicU64`.
+
+| Bit(s) | Meaning |
+| --- | --- |
+| bit 0 | tag: `0 = unswizzled`, `1 = swizzled` |
+| bits 1..63 (unswizzled) | `PageId` encoded as `page_id << 1` |
+| bits 1..63 (swizzled) | aligned frame address with bit 0 clear |
+
+Constraints:
+- `PageId <= (u64::MAX >> 1)`.
+- Swizzled frame addresses must be aligned so bit 0 is always clear.
+
+### Rust Prototype Surface
+
+```rust
+pub enum SwizzleState {
+    Unswizzled { page_id: u64 },
+    Swizzled { frame_addr: u64 },
+}
+
+pub enum PageTemperature { Hot, Cooling, Cold }
+
+pub struct SwizzlePtr { /* AtomicU64 */ }
+```
+
+The prototype provides:
+- `new_unswizzled`, `new_swizzled`.
+- `try_swizzle(expected_page_id, frame_addr)` (CAS).
+- `try_unswizzle(expected_frame_addr, page_id)` (CAS).
+- temperature transition validation.
+
+## Swizzle/Unswizzle Protocol
+
+### Swizzle
+
+Preconditions:
+1. Target page is resident in buffer pool and has stable frame address.
+2. Parent-child mapping confirms `parent_ptr == unswizzled(page_id)`.
+3. Caller holds appropriate latch/ownership for parent page pointer array edit.
+
+Operation:
+1. Encode expected unswizzled word (`page_id << 1`).
+2. Encode replacement swizzled word (`frame_addr | 1`).
+3. `compare_exchange(expected, replacement, AcqRel, Acquire)`.
+4. On CAS success, pointer becomes swizzled atomically for all readers.
+5. On CAS failure, re-read state and retry or abort path based on caller policy.
+
+### Unswizzle
+
+Preconditions:
+1. Eviction candidate frame is in COOLING state.
+2. Parent references to candidate are enumerable (reverse-map / parent index).
+3. EBR grace period protection is available.
+
+Operation:
+1. Mark frame COOLING.
+2. For each parent reference, CAS from expected swizzled word to unswizzled `PageId`.
+3. Publish unswizzle completion.
+4. Wait for EBR grace period so readers with old pointer views drain.
+5. Transition frame to COLD and allow physical eviction.
+
+Safety property:
+- No reader may observe freed frame memory if unswizzle publication + grace period completion occur before reclamation.
+
+## Concurrency Safety Analysis
+
+### Reader Safety Contract
+
+Readers:
+1. Load tagged word atomically (`Acquire`).
+2. If unswizzled, route through normal page lookup.
+3. If swizzled, treat frame as protected by current epoch pin.
+
+Writers/evictors:
+1. Perform pointer transition with `AcqRel` CAS.
+2. Never free frame memory until EBR reports grace-period completion.
+
+This enforces a no-dangling-follow contract under lock-free reads.
+
+### Failure Modes
+
+| Failure mode | Detection | Fallback |
+| --- | --- | --- |
+| CAS contention on pointer transition | CAS returns observed value | Retry with bounded attempts or leave unswizzled |
+| Misaligned frame address | encode validation failure | reject transition, leave unswizzled |
+| Parent index incomplete during unswizzle | transition audit mismatch | block eviction, keep COOLING, retry repair |
+| EBR unavailable/degraded | health check / capability missing | force unswizzled-only mode |
+
+Deterministic fallback behavior is always legal: keep references unswizzled and continue using PageId lookup.
+
+## Root Pinning
+
+Root pages for each B-tree remain permanently HOT and swizzled:
+- Root frames are excluded from eviction candidate lists.
+- Root pin decisions are emitted at `INFO` level.
+- On recovery, root frames are re-established lazily on first access and then pinned.
+
+## HOT/COOLING/COLD State Machine
+
+Allowed transitions:
+- `HOT -> COOLING`
+- `COOLING -> HOT` (rescued by access)
+- `COOLING -> COLD` (eviction path)
+- `COLD -> HOT` (fault and reload)
+- identity transitions allowed
+
+Forbidden direct transition:
+- `HOT -> COLD` (must pass through COOLING)
+
+```mermaid
+stateDiagram-v2
+    [*] --> HOT
+    HOT --> COOLING: eviction candidate
+    COOLING --> HOT: access hit / rescue
+    COOLING --> COLD: unswizzle + grace period complete
+    COLD --> HOT: page fault + reload + reswizzle
+```
+
+## Buffer Pool and EBR Integration Points
+
+Buffer-pool integration:
+1. Parent-reference index maintenance for child pointers.
+2. Eviction scanner consults `PageTemperature`.
+3. Eviction finalizer calls unswizzle protocol before frame reclaim.
+
+EBR integration:
+1. Reader path pins epoch before swizzled dereference.
+2. Evictor retires frame memory only after grace period.
+3. Epoch pressure metrics feed cooling window tuning.
+
+## Recovery Protocol
+
+On process crash/restart:
+1. In-memory frame pointers are invalid by definition.
+2. All child refs start unswizzled (PageId form).
+3. Normal page loads progressively re-swizzle hot references.
+4. Root pages are re-pinned when loaded.
+
+No on-disk format changes are required for this protocol.
+
+## Iterator API and Ownership Boundaries
+
+Proposed iterator interaction:
+- Cursor descent reads `SwizzlePtr`.
+- Iterator remains agnostic to encoding details via `SwizzleState`.
+- Pointer transition ownership belongs to buffer-pool/eviction subsystem, not cursor.
+
+Ownership boundaries:
+- `fsqlite-btree`: tagged pointer type + transition semantics.
+- buffer pool: residency policy + parent-reference index + eviction scheduling.
+- MVCC/EBR: grace period enforcement before reclaim.
+
+## Structured Logging Contract
+
+Required fields for swizzle events:
+- `trace_id`
+- `run_id`
+- `scenario_id`
+- `bead_id`
+- `op` (`swizzle` | `unswizzle` | `temperature_transition` | `root_pin`)
+- `page_id`
+- `frame_addr` (nullable for unswizzled-only events)
+- `from_state` / `to_state` (for temperature transitions)
+- `outcome` (`pass` | `fail` | `retry`)
+- `duration_us`
+- `error_code` (nullable)
+
+Log levels:
+- `TRACE`: swizzle/unswizzle high-frequency events.
+- `DEBUG`: HOT/COOLING/COLD transitions.
+- `INFO`: root pinning and unswizzled-only fallback mode toggles.
+
+Conformance check:
+- E2E pilot script validates required JSON fields are present and non-empty where mandated.
+
+## Pilot E2E Scenario
+
+Scenario artifact:
+- Script: `e2e/bd_2uza4_1_swizzle_protocol_pilot.sh`
+- Deterministic seed: `2026021501`
+- Replay command emitted in summary JSON
+- Artifacts: run log + JSONL summary + SHA256 digests
+
+Pilot purpose:
+- Prove prototype state transitions and CAS semantics behave deterministically.
+- Validate structured logging schema for the swizzle protocol surface.

 - **Evidence**: `crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_2d3i_1_ssi_witness_plane_deterministic_scenarios_compliance.rs`
 
+### E2E-CNC-006: Pointer swizzle protocol pilot (tagged CAS transitions)
+- **Description**: Validate deterministic swizzle/unswizzle CAS transitions and HOT/COOLING/COLD state contract for B-tree child references.
+- **Pass criteria**: Swizzle prototype tests pass, structured log schema fields validate, replay command reproduces run.
+- **Invariants**: INV-C2 (No Deadlock), swizzle protocol design invariants in `docs/design/pointer-swizzle-protocol.md`
+- **Evidence**: `e2e/bd_2uza4_1_swizzle_protocol_pilot.sh`, `crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs` tests
+

@@ -0,0 +1,147 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+BEAD_ID="bd-2uza4.1"
+SCENARIO_ID="E2E-CNC-006"
+SEED=2026021501
+WORKSPACE_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+REPORT_ROOT="${WORKSPACE_ROOT}/artifacts/swizzle_protocol_pilot"
+RUN_ID="${BEAD_ID}-$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)-$$"
+TRACE_ID="${RUN_ID}"
+REPORT_JSONL="${REPORT_ROOT}/${RUN_ID}.jsonl"
+LOG_PATH="${REPORT_ROOT}/${RUN_ID}.log"
+LOG_SCHEMA_REF="docs/e2e_log_schema_contract.md"
+JSON_OUTPUT=false
+
+if [[ "${1:-}" == "--json" ]]; then
+    JSON_OUTPUT=true
+fi
+
+TEST_CMD=(cargo test -p fsqlite-btree swizzle -- --nocapture)
+if command -v rch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    TEST_CMD=(rch exec -- "${TEST_CMD[@]}")
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "${REPORT_ROOT}"
+: >"${REPORT_JSONL}"
+
+emit_event() {
+    local phase="$1"
+    local event_type="$2"
+    local op="$3"
+    local outcome="$4"
+    local duration_ms="$5"
+    local error_code="$6"
+    local log_sha256="$7"
+    jq -nc \
+        --arg trace_id "${TRACE_ID}" \
+        --arg run_id "${RUN_ID}" \
+        --arg scenario_id "${SCENARIO_ID}" \
+        --arg bead_id "${BEAD_ID}" \
+        --arg phase "${phase}" \
+        --arg event_type "${event_type}" \
+        --arg op "${op}" \
+        --arg outcome "${outcome}" \
+        --argjson duration_ms "${duration_ms}" \
+        --arg error_code "${error_code}" \
+        --arg log_path "${LOG_PATH}" \
+        --arg log_sha256 "${log_sha256}" \
+        --argjson seed "${SEED}" \
+        --arg schema_ref "${LOG_SCHEMA_REF}" \
+        '{
+            trace_id: $trace_id,
+            run_id: $run_id,
+            scenario_id: $scenario_id,
+            bead_id: $bead_id,
+            phase: $phase,
+            event_type: $event_type,
+            op: $op,
+            page_id: null,
+            frame_addr: null,
+            from_state: null,
+            to_state: null,
+            outcome: $outcome,
+            duration_ms: $duration_ms,
+            duration_us: ($duration_ms * 1000),
+            error_code: (if $error_code == "null" then null else $error_code end),
+            seed: $seed,
+            artifact_paths: [$log_path],
+            log_sha256: $log_sha256,
+            schema_ref: $schema_ref
+        }' >>"${REPORT_JSONL}"
+}
+
+printf 'bead_id=%s level=INFO run_id=%s trace_id=%s scenario_id=%s phase=start seed=%s reference=%s\n' \
+    "${BEAD_ID}" "${RUN_ID}" "${TRACE_ID}" "${SCENARIO_ID}" "${SEED}" "${LOG_SCHEMA_REF}"
+
+start_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+set +e
+(
+    cd "${WORKSPACE_ROOT}"
+    RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 "${TEST_CMD[@]}"
+) >"${LOG_PATH}" 2>&1
+exit_code=$?
+set -e
+end_ns="$(date +%s%N)"
+
+duration_ms="$(( (end_ns - start_ns) / 1000000 ))"
+log_sha256="$(sha256sum "${LOG_PATH}" | awk '{print $1}')"
+
+if [[ ${exit_code} -eq 0 ]]; then
+    emit_event "execute" "pilot_result" "swizzle" "pass" "${duration_ms}" "null" "${log_sha256}"
+else
+    emit_event "execute" "pilot_result" "swizzle" "fail" "${duration_ms}" "E_EXIT_${exit_code}" "${log_sha256}"
+fi
+
+if ! jq -e '
+    has("trace_id")
+    and has("run_id")
+    and has("scenario_id")
+    and has("bead_id")
+    and has("op")
+    and has("outcome")
+    and has("duration_us")
+    and has("seed")
+    and has("error_code")
+' "${REPORT_JSONL}" >/dev/null; then
+    printf 'bead_id=%s level=ERROR run_id=%s trace_id=%s scenario_id=%s phase=validate case=log_schema_conformance_failed report=%s\n' \
+        "${BEAD_ID}" "${RUN_ID}" "${TRACE_ID}" "${SCENARIO_ID}" "${REPORT_JSONL}"
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+summary_outcome="pass"
+summary_error="null"
+if [[ ${exit_code} -ne 0 ]]; then
+    summary_outcome="fail"
+    summary_error="E_EXIT_${exit_code}"
+fi
+emit_event "summary" "run_summary" "swizzle" "${summary_outcome}" "${duration_ms}" "${summary_error}" "${log_sha256}"
+
+printf 'bead_id=%s level=INFO run_id=%s trace_id=%s scenario_id=%s phase=summary outcome=%s duration_ms=%s log=%s report=%s log_sha256=%s\n' \
+    "${BEAD_ID}" "${RUN_ID}" "${TRACE_ID}" "${SCENARIO_ID}" "${summary_outcome}" "${duration_ms}" "${LOG_PATH}" "${REPORT_JSONL}" "${log_sha256}"
+
+if ${JSON_OUTPUT}; then
+    jq -nc \
+        --arg bead_id "${BEAD_ID}" \
+        --arg run_id "${RUN_ID}" \
+        --arg trace_id "${TRACE_ID}" \
+        --arg scenario_id "${SCENARIO_ID}" \
+        --arg outcome "${summary_outcome}" \
+        --arg report_jsonl "${REPORT_JSONL}" \
+        --arg log_path "${LOG_PATH}" \
+        --arg log_sha256 "${log_sha256}" \
+        --arg replay "RUST_TEST_THREADS=1 ${TEST_CMD[*]}" \
+        '{
+            bead_id: $bead_id,
+            run_id: $run_id,
+            trace_id: $trace_id,
+            scenario_id: $scenario_id,
+            outcome: $outcome,
+            report_jsonl: $report_jsonl,
+            log_path: $log_path,
+            log_sha256: $log_sha256,
+            replay: $replay
+        }'
+fi
+
+exit "${exit_code}"
